Integrate Banuba Face AR SDK using the official @banuba/react-native module, replacing the "AR Lenses coming soon" placeholder. This is a CLEAN integration — DeepAR has been fully removed already.
Banuba client token (store in .env, NOT hardcoded):
BANUBA_CLIENT_
Steps:

Read Banuba's official React Native integration docs first — search for @banuba/react-native on npm and check their GitHub repo for the latest setup guide (Expo compatibility section especially).
Install: pnpm add @banuba/react-native
Add the client token to .env and eas.json (both preview + production profiles), following the same pattern used for the previous DeepAR keys.
Create a config plugin (or use Banuba's official Expo plugin if one exists in their package) for:

Android: Banuba SDK dependency + permissions
iOS: Banuba pod + Info.plist permissions


Create BanubaCameraView.tsx wrapping Banuba's camera component:

Initialize with client token from env
Expose applyEffect(effectName) method
Falls back gracefully with "Lenses require a dev build" message in Expo Go


Replace the "✨ AR Lenses coming soon" placeholder in LensOverlay.tsx with BanubaCameraView.
CRITICAL — verify capture still works: takePictureAsync/video recording must continue working via Banuba's capture methods (or fall back to expo-camera when no effect is active, whichever Banuba's architecture supports). Test passthrough mode FIRST before adding effects.
Download 2-3 free effects from Banuba's effect library/asset store to test with.
expo prebuild --clean then report readiness for EAS build — confirm before running.

Report the plan and any New Architecture compatibility notes BEFORE writing code (Banuba reportedly supports New Arch, so newArchEnabled: true might be fine this time — confirm from docs).
